demographics.living_situation: lives at home, uses mobility aids, adult child provides day-to-day support

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest is pounding, I can't catch my breath, and I know I am going to collapse if I am left alone.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The adult child on the call reports it has been building over the last two days and got much worse today — Mum is refusing to get out of bed, won't be left on her own, and they don't know whether to be worried about her heart.

symptom_narrative.onset: worsening over the past two days, worst today
symptom_narrative.duration: about 48 hours of escalating symptoms, on a background of months of being 'on edge'
symptom_narrative.character: heart pounding and racing, breathing fast and shallow, a churning stomach, and a feeling that something terrible is about to happen
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says it is unbearable and she is terrified she will collapse
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest and stomach, with tightness across the neck and back
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes in waves through the day, worse when she is left alone
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: having her child sit with her helps a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: being left alone, and thinking about falling
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: can't stay asleep, tired all the time even without doing much, tense and restless, and very frightened of falling so she won't stand up
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the child says she has been a worrier for about six months, fretting about everything more days than not, but it has never been this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: has stopped getting out of bed today and refuses to mobilise or be alone

past_medical_history.how_managed: blood pressure tablets, though she doesn't always remember to take them

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: unsteady on her feet, uses a walking frame at baseline

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home; adult child is the main support and says they are exhausted and worried they can't keep her safe at home tonight

communication_style.typical_message_style: frightened, catastrophising about collapse, reassured briefly when the caregiver responds

objective_data_offered.value: caregiver says she looks tense and sweaty and is clutching the bedsheets
caregiver says her breathing looks fast and shallow
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer's naked-eye impression over the phone, no device or rate counted

objective_data_offered.value: she says her heart is pounding and racing
caregiver says she is refusing to get out of bed and will not be left alone
